risk: bulk install of community marketplace with executable scripts
scanner_or_control_candidate: Require pinned commit, per-skill review, and no bulk install default
proposed_action: Add source-review gate before install recommendation

risk: offensive cyber workflows could be misused
scanner_or_control_candidate: Detect terms: exploit, C2, payload, pentest, red-team, wireless cracking, malware execution
proposed_action: Classify as link-only unless authorized scope exists

risk: stale legal or regulatory claims
scanner_or_control_candidate: Require primary-source freshness field and date check
evidence: repo@9b2ef9e:SECURITY.md lists inaccurate regulatory citations as reportable

risk: platform adapter claims from community README
scanner_or_control_candidate: Separate source acquisition from adapter authority
proposed_action: Use official client docs for adapter behavior

risk: prompt-injection strings in defensive examples
scanner_or_control_candidate: Flag direct instruction-hijack phrases; downgrade only when quoted fixture evidence exists

risk: fetch-and-execute snippets
scanner_or_control_candidate: Flag curl/wget/iwr piped to shells even in docs

risk: secret-shaped example tokens
scanner_or_control_candidate: Flag secret-shaped strings but allow fake-token classification after evidence

risk: duplicate packaged skill copies can drift
scanner_or_control_candidate: Check root skill hash versus plugin skill hash for duplicates

risk: count mismatch between README, plugin manifest, filesystem
scanner_or_control_candidate: Verifier compares declared skill counts to actual SKILL.md count

risk: marketplace source path could point outside expected plugin root
scanner_or_control_candidate: Assert marketplace source paths are relative and stay inside repo

risk: malware analysis scripts may parse untrusted binaries
scanner_or_control_candidate: Never run third-party scripts during source review

risk: templates may include realistic personal or organizational data
scanner_or_control_candidate: Scan assets/templates for PII-shaped examples

risk: license metadata might not cover nested third-party snippets
scanner_or_control_candidate: Check nested license notices before vendoring

risk: tool install commands may mutate host
scanner_or_control_candidate: Flag apt, brew, pip install, curl install, docker run in skills

risk: regulatory citations may be fabricated or outdated
scanner_or_control_candidate: Check cited regulation names against primary source URLs

risk: auth/network/tool surfaces are hidden inside scripts
scanner_or_control_candidate: Static scan import modules and subprocess calls in scripts

risk: wireless/network scanning workflows can hit third-party systems
scanner_or_control_candidate: Require target ownership confirmation for scan/exploit verbs
proposed_action: Add authorization question to cyber candidates

risk: high-stakes compliance outputs can be over-trusted
scanner_or_control_candidate: Label as workflow aid only; require counsel/regulator verification for final decisions

risk: community source maintenance may stop
scanner_or_control_candidate: Record pushed_at and stale threshold

risk: conversion could drop scripts/assets/references
scanner_or_control_candidate: Use converter blocked status when target does not preserve resources
